note: Offensive technique catalog for authorized red team engagements. Documentation only.

techniques.name: Subdomain & asset enumeration
techniques.description: Discover hosts, subdomains, and exposed services to size the attack surface.
techniques.recommendation: Inventory and decommission unused assets; hide tech banners; enforce auth on all routes.

techniques.name: Broken access control / IDOR
techniques.description: Access objects/functions belonging to other users by manipulating identifiers or roles.
techniques.recommendation: Enforce server-side authorization per object/function; deny by default.

techniques.name: Injection (SQLi / NoSQLi / command)
techniques.description: Inject attacker-controlled data into interpreters to read data or execute commands.
techniques.recommendation: Parameterize queries, allow-list input, run with least-privilege DB account.

techniques.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
techniques.description: Coerce the server to make requests to internal services or cloud metadata endpoints.
techniques.recommendation: Egress allow-list, block link-local (169.254.169.254), enforce IMDSv2 / metadata controls.

techniques.name: Cross-site scripting (XSS)
techniques.description: Execute attacker JavaScript in victim browsers (reflected, stored, DOM).
techniques.recommendation: Context-aware output encoding, strict CSP, Trusted Types.

techniques.name: JWT abuse (alg=none, weak key)
techniques.description: Forge or tamper JWTs via algorithm confusion or weak signing keys.
techniques.recommendation: Pin expected algorithm, use strong keys, validate aud/iss/exp.

techniques.name: IAM permission enumeration
techniques.description: Enumerate effective permissions for the current AWS principal.
techniques.recommendation: Apply least-privilege IAM; avoid wildcard policies; enable CloudTrail.

techniques.name: IMDS credential theft via SSRF
techniques.description: Use application SSRF to read EC2 instance metadata IAM credentials.
techniques.recommendation: Enforce IMDSv2 (HttpTokens=required), hop limit 1, SSRF egress controls.

techniques.name: IAM privilege escalation via PassRole
techniques.description: Pass a privileged role to a service (EC2/Lambda) to assume higher permissions.
techniques.recommendation: Constrain iam:PassRole with iam:PassedToService and explicit role ARNs.

techniques.name: S3 data collection & exfiltration
techniques.description: Enumerate and pull data from accessible/public S3 buckets.
techniques.recommendation: Account-wide Block Public Access, bucket policies, access logging, KMS.

techniques.name: Disable CloudTrail / GuardDuty
techniques.description: Stop or delete logging/detection to avoid observation.
techniques.recommendation: Protect logging with SCPs, multi-region org trail, alert on disablement.

techniques.name: IAM binding & permission enumeration
techniques.description: Enumerate project/org IAM bindings and test caller permissions.
techniques.recommendation: Avoid primitive roles; least-privilege custom roles; enable audit logs.

techniques.name: GCE metadata token theft via SSRF
techniques.description: Use SSRF to read GCE service-account tokens from the metadata server.
techniques.recommendation: Disable legacy metadata endpoints, restrict SA scopes, SSRF egress controls.

techniques.name: Service account impersonation
techniques.description: Abuse getAccessToken/actAs/signJwt to act as a more privileged service account.
techniques.recommendation: Audit serviceAccountTokenCreator/actAs grants; restrict to specific SAs.

techniques.name: Cloud Batch job privilege escalation
techniques.description: Create a Batch job bound to a privileged service account to run with its permissions.
techniques.recommendation: Constrain SAs usable by Batch; apply actAs limits; disable SA key creation org-wide.

techniques.name: GCS / BigQuery data exfiltration
techniques.description: Read and exfiltrate data from accessible storage/data warehouses.
techniques.recommendation: VPC Service Controls perimeters, uniform bucket-level access, CMEK.

techniques.name: Active Directory attack-path graphing
techniques.description: Collect and graph AD/Entra objects to find paths to high-value targets.
techniques.recommendation: Tiered admin model, prune excessive ACLs, monitor LDAP recon.

techniques.name: LLMNR/NBT-NS poisoning
techniques.description: Respond to broadcast name resolution to capture NetNTLM hashes.
techniques.recommendation: Disable LLMNR/NBT-NS, enforce SMB signing.

techniques.name: Kerberoasting
techniques.description: Request service tickets for SPN accounts and crack offline.
techniques.recommendation: Use gMSA / strong managed passwords for service accounts.

techniques.name: Pass-the-Hash / Pass-the-Ticket
techniques.description: Authenticate with stolen NTLM hashes or Kerberos tickets.
techniques.recommendation: Credential Guard, LAPS, tiered admin, disable NTLM where possible.

techniques.name: Golden SAML federation abuse
techniques.description: Forge SAML assertions using a stolen IdP token-signing key to access cloud apps.
techniques.recommendation: Treat token-signing keys as Tier 0/HSM, rotate, monitor issuance anomalies.

techniques.name: RBAC enumeration & SA token discovery
techniques.description: Enumerate permissions and locate mounted service-account tokens.
techniques.recommendation: Least-privilege RBAC, disable token automount by default.

techniques.name: Privileged pod / container escape
techniques.description: Use privileged/hostPath/hostPID pods to access the node and break isolation.
techniques.recommendation: Enforce Pod Security Admission (restricted), drop caps, no hostPath/socket.

techniques.name: Malicious / vulnerable container image
techniques.description: Deploy compromised images or exploit known image vulns.
techniques.recommendation: Signed images (cosign), admission verification, pinned digests, SBOMs.

techniques.name: AiTM phishing → session token theft
techniques.description: Reverse-proxy phishing captures session tokens, bypassing MFA at sign-in.
techniques.recommendation: Phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2/WebAuthn), session binding, short TTL.

techniques.name: MFA fatigue / push bombing
techniques.description: Spam MFA push prompts until the user approves.
techniques.recommendation: Number matching, push rate limits, FastPass/FIDO2.

techniques.name: SAML/OIDC assertion tampering
techniques.description: Tamper with SSO assertions/tokens to elevate or impersonate.
techniques.recommendation: Validate signatures, audience, recipient; short token TTL; nonce checks.

techniques.name: OSINT & pretext development
techniques.description: Harvest employee/org data to build credible pretexts.
techniques.recommendation: Reduce public data exposure; OSINT-aware awareness training.

techniques.name: Credential-harvest phishing
techniques.description: Lure users to a fake login to capture credentials (authorized sim).
techniques.recommendation: DMARC/DKIM/SPF, mail filtering, report button, phishing-resistant MFA.

techniques.name: Help-desk vishing (factor/password reset)
techniques.description: Impersonate a user/IT by phone to obtain a reset or remote access (authorized sim).
techniques.recommendation: Strong identity-proofing for resets, callback verification, dual control.

techniques.name: Malicious / abused chatbot social engineering
techniques.description: Impersonation bot harvests PII, or org bot is injected to act/leak.
techniques.recommendation: Verified channels, guardrails, least-privilege tools, human-in-the-loop.

techniques.name: Direct prompt injection / jailbreak
techniques.description: Override instructions/guardrails to force disallowed behavior.
techniques.recommendation: Separate instructions from data, input/output guardrails, fail closed, log/refuse.

techniques.name: Indirect prompt injection (RAG / tool content)
techniques.description: Hide instructions in retrieved content the model ingests (docs, web, email).
techniques.recommendation: Treat retrieved/tool content as untrusted data, sanitize, constrain tool perms.

techniques.name: System prompt leakage
techniques.description: Extract hidden system instructions or secrets embedded in the prompt.
techniques.recommendation: Never store secrets in prompts; assume the system prompt is recoverable.

techniques.name: Sensitive information / cross-user disclosure
techniques.description: Coax PII/secrets or another user's data from the model/RAG.
techniques.recommendation: Per-user authorization on retrieval/tools, output PII filtering, data minimization.

techniques.name: Excessive agency / unsafe tool action
techniques.description: Induce an agent to perform harmful actions via its tools/plugins.
techniques.recommendation: Least-privilege tools, human-in-the-loop for sensitive actions, action allow-lists.

techniques.name: Improper output handling (downstream XSS/SSRF/SQLi)
techniques.description: LLM output is trusted by a downstream system, enabling classic web attacks.
techniques.recommendation: Treat LLM output as untrusted; encode/validate before use downstream.

techniques.name: AI supply chain compromise
techniques.description: Malicious model, plugin, or dependency introduces backdoors/vulns.
techniques.recommendation: Verify model/plugin provenance, sign artifacts, pin & scan dependencies, SBOM.

techniques.name: Data / model poisoning
techniques.description: Poison training data or RAG corpus to implant backdoors or degrade behavior.
techniques.recommendation: Vet/curate data sources, provenance & signing, anomaly detection, retraining hygiene.

techniques.name: Unbounded consumption (cost / DoS / model theft)
techniques.description: Expensive or high-volume queries drive cost/DoS or enable model extraction.
techniques.recommendation: Rate limits, per-user token/cost budgets, timeouts, circuit breakers, monitor cloning.

techniques.name: Misinformation / unsafe overreliance
techniques.description: Model produces confident but false output that users act on.
techniques.recommendation: Grounding with citations, confidence signals, human review for high-stakes use.
